description: Negative SHACL fixtures. Each file is crafted to violate a specific shape. The gate asserts the expected result actually fires — matched on focus node, constraint component, path and severity, plus a message substring — not merely that the graph fails to conform. See README.md.
notes: sourceShape is a BLANK NODE for every inline sh:property shape in music_vocabulary_shapes.ttl, so it cannot be matched by IRI. Match on the tuple below instead.

`expect` is a LIST: a fixture may legitimately break more than one thing at once (a record label credited as a producer is neither a :MusicalAgent nor a role-holder, and both failures are real). Any Violation NOT declared here fails the gate.

sh:node yields TWO results: the outer NodeConstraintComponent on the crediting work, and the inner HasValueConstraintComponent on the agent. Both are declared — the inner one is the precise statement of what is wrong ('this agent does not hold the role').

fixtures.why: A record label claiming a career. THE fixture that was once claimed to exist and did not — a previous release said this shape was 'verified with a negative test' when no such test was ever committed. It is also what proves the taxonomy-only ont_graph is load-bearing: hand the RDFS closure the `:startsCareerIn rdfs:domain :MusicalAgent` axiom and this file CONFORMS, because the closure retypes the label into the very class the shape checks for.

A musician who plays nothing. Expected severity is Warning, not Violation — the completeness rule carries sh:severity sh:Warning. A gate keyed on Violation count would pass this while looking rigorous. Since v3.0.0 there is no conductor exemption: a conductor who plays nothing is a :MusicalPerson, not a :Musician, so this shape never targets them.

expect.message_contains: should play at least one instrument

fixtures.why: A valid :MusicalAgent, credited as producer, who holds no :ProducerRole. Since the role left rdfs:range (where RDFS would have invented it) it must be asserted — and this proves it is checked.
Adversarial: the agent DOES hold a role — :ProducerRole — just not the one being credited. A shape that merely checked 'has some :hasRole' would pass this.
A performing musician with an instrument, credited as composer. Playing a work is not writing it — the distinction roles exist to draw.

A song written by a record label. Breaks BOTH arms: a label is not a :MusicalAgent, and holds no :LyricistRole. Type-check and role-check live in separate property shapes precisely so a failure says which.
An album produced by a record label. Labels release records; they do not produce them. Breaks both the type and the role arm.
A composition composed by a record label. Breaks both the type and the role arm.
A root genre claiming a parent. Would silently break the :hasGenre/:hasBroaderGenre* roll-up behind CQ-1 and CQ-9.

expect.message_contains: must not have a broader genre

fixtures.why: A peak chart position with no chart named is a meaningless number (CQ-10). Proves the SPARQL-constraint shapes fire, not just the property shapes.
An artist who retires eleven years before starting. Would corrupt CQ-13's era-overlap answers.

expect.message_contains: must not be later than activeUntil

fixtures.why: An untyped string where a gYear belongs — one of the style guide's five ruthless rules, and CQ-13 compares these years numerically.

expect.message_contains: activeFrom must be a single xsd:gYear
